_metadata.description: NGAI agent template with tier guidance; keep it concise and delete unused optional sections.
_metadata.instructions: Fill only what you need. Keep Tier 1 for simple agents; add Tier 2/3 if warranted.
_metadata.usage: JSON = structured schema; MD = narrative. Delete sections not used.

tier_guidance.tier_1_core: Essential for any agent (agent_type, implementation for chosen type, io_contract, primary_data if data-driven, dependencies, validation)
tier_guidance.tier_2_recommended: For production/shared agents (operational_guidance, error_handling, logging_observability, configuration)
tier_guidance.tier_3_optional: Only if needed (common_patterns, domain_specific_patterns, decision_rules, constraints, tests, cross_references, changelog)

agent_details.description: One-sentence description of what this agent does.

agent_type.description: Select exactly one; delete irrelevant implementation branches.
agent_type.notes: Choosing a type helps decide which optional sections to keep.

rule_based.default_action: What happens if no rules match

inputs.name: input_name
inputs.file_filter: Optional. Comma-separated glob patterns (e.g. '*.docx,*.pdf'). Used only when type='folder' to limit which files in the folder AgentJ invokes the agent on. Empty/absent = all files.

io_contract._non_interactive_mode_doc: Set true ONLY after the agent has been validated interactively. Required when true: alert_channel below. See knowledge/behavioral_discipline.json → non_interactive_mode for graduation rules and per-principle surface mechanisms.
io_contract._alert_channel_doc: Required when non_interactive_mode is true. Slack webhook URL, monitoring endpoint, email address, or error log path — wherever P-003 (Stop on Defect) should surface in non-interactive mode. make_agent_qc flags BD-QC-007 critical if missing.

behavioral_discipline._description: Cross-cutting behavioral discipline every agent inherits. Source of truth: knowledge/behavioral_discipline.md (narrative) and knowledge/behavioral_discipline.json (structured rules, principles P-001 through P-010).
behavioral_discipline._required_in_md: Every agent's MD MUST contain a `## Behavioral Discipline (core)` section, embedded immediately after `## Key Principles (core)`, populated using compact_boilerplate.md_section_template from knowledge/behavioral_discipline.json.
behavioral_discipline._required_in_system_prompt: Every llm_agent's implementation.llm_agent.system_prompt MUST contain the discipline boilerplate, embedded using compact_boilerplate.system_prompt_template from knowledge/behavioral_discipline.json.
behavioral_discipline._interaction_pattern_doc: Drives which principles get embedded. Distinct from agent_type.type — interaction_pattern is the BEHAVIORAL pattern (how the agent interacts with users and external systems); agent_type.type is the IMPLEMENTATION pattern (how it's built). The two are orthogonal — an agent's interaction_pattern depends on its workflow shape, NOT on its implementation type. Examples (note opposite shapes from the same implementation): an api_agent webhook handler with one fire-and-done call → single_call_api; an api_agent changelog generator that hits a GitHub API across many PRs → multi_step_batch. An llm_agent doc-Q&A bot → read_only; an llm_agent data-migration assistant → multi_step_batch. Look up the applicable principles in knowledge/behavioral_discipline.json → agent_type_applicability.types[<interaction_pattern>] using the decision flow in make_agent.md → '## Behavioral Discipline (core)' → integration step 2.
behavioral_discipline._applicable_principles_doc: Array of principle IDs (P-001 through P-010) embedded in this agent's discipline section. Populated from agent_type_applicability.types[<interaction_pattern>].always_include. The four no-override principles (P-001, P-003, P-007, P-010) MUST always be present regardless of interaction_pattern.
behavioral_discipline._override_decisions_doc: Array of {principle_id, reason} for any principle the agent skips or partially applies. Populated only if a principle is overridden. Empty if the discipline applies cleanly. Per the hard rule, every override must have an articulated reason.

primary_data.description: Core structured data (prompts, mappings, rules, endpoints)

validation.description: How to verify the agent works

discipline_validation._description: Behavioral discipline checks every generated agent must pass. Canonical rules live in knowledge/behavioral_discipline.json → qc_checks (BD-QC-001 through BD-QC-007). make_agent_qc rules 17 (delegates to BD-QC-001 through BD-QC-006) and 18 (delegates to BD-QC-007) enforce these. Surfaced here so a JSON-only consumer of this template sees the discipline contract without crossing into make_agent_qc.
required_checks: BD-QC-001 — `## Behavioral Discipline (core)` section present in agent MD
BD-QC-002 — discipline boilerplate present in implementation.llm_agent.system_prompt
BD-QC-003 — applicable_principles match interaction_pattern's always_include array
BD-QC-004 — at least one trust marker in validation.test_cases output
BD-QC-005 — every override has a documented reason in override_decisions
BD-QC-006 — P-001, P-003, P-007, P-010 always present (CRITICAL — never skip)
BD-QC-007 — alert_channel declared if non_interactive_mode is true (CRITICAL when applicable)

commands: make_agent_qc — validate this spec via the QC meta-skill in make_agent_qc.md/.json. Conversational invocation: 'Use make_agent_qc to validate <agent>.md and <agent>.json. Apply all 18 rules. Report critical issues first.' make_agent_qc rules 17 and 18 enforce the discipline_validation.required_checks above. Score must be ≥95 for a passing agent; critical issues fail the agent regardless of score.

best_practices.description: Instrument on_tool_call, on_tool_result, and on_agent_end hooks for observability; use hooks for metrics, logging, and guardrail triggers rather than embedding logic in tools
Keep tool count to 10-20 per agent; beyond this threshold model performance degrades. Split large tool sets across specialized sub-agents.

Use low temperature (0.0–0.2) for agents that rely on deterministic tool selection and parameter generation. Higher temperatures increase tool call variability — the model may choose different tools or generate different parameter values for identical inputs. Conversational agents can use higher temperatures (0.7+) for natural variation. Document the temperature choice in implementation.llm_agent.parameters with a note on why. Exception: Gemini 3 models are tuned at the default temperature of 1.0 and Google explicitly warns that lowering it (including to 0.1–0.2) may cause looping or degraded performance on complex reasoning and math-heavy tasks; for Gemini 3 tool-calling agents, leave temperature at 1.0 unless a measured benefit justifies overriding the default, and record the model + chosen value in implementation.llm_agent.parameters.

known_failures.cause: Model repeatedly calls tools without reaching a stopping condition
known_failures.mitigation: Set max_turns limit; use tool_choice=auto (not required) after first tool call; implement loop detection in tool runner

known_failures.cause: Server-side sampling loop has a default limit of 10 iterations; when reached, API returns stop_reason: 'pause_turn' instead of a final answer
known_failures.mitigation: On receiving stop_reason: 'pause_turn', resend the API response as a new user message to allow the agent to continue and complete its task

known_failures.cause: Very large or deeply nested JSON schemas provided to structured output or tool parameter definitions are silently rejected or cause API errors
known_failures.mitigation: Flatten nested schemas where possible; split complex types into multiple simpler tools or output types; validate schema against platform limits before deploying

fallbacks.description: When token usage approaches the context window limit during a long-running agentic task, enable automatic compaction (context summarization) to allow the task to continue without losing progress. Alternative: pause and resume via session ID if the platform supports it.

patterns.name: tool_use_behavior
patterns.description: Control whether the agent continues after a tool call or uses the tool result as the final response.

before.description: Default: LLM processes tool results and generates a follow-up response (agentic loop continues)

after.description: First tool output becomes the final response — useful when the tool result IS the answer (e.g., database lookup)

patterns.notes: Four approaches: 'run_llm_again' (default agentic loop), 'stop_on_first_tool' (tool result = final response), StopAtToolName (stop when specific tool called), custom function (inspect tool results and decide dynamically).

patterns.name: memory_state_strategy
patterns.description: Choose stateless, manual-history, or platform-managed session memory before building the agent — retrofitting later forces changes in system prompt, tool runner, or surrounding application.

before.description: Strategy unspecified; runtime defaults inconsistently across calls; multi-turn behavior emerges accidentally.

after.description: Strategy declared at spec time; runtime persists exactly what the strategy requires; session lifecycle is auditable.

patterns.notes: stateless = each call independent (right for single_call_api). manual_history = application appends prior turns (right for cross-provider portability). platform_session = store a session ID and resume (right for multi_step_batch and long-running interactive agents). Anthropic: resume=session_id, fork=session_id. OpenAI: Sessions primitive with SQLAlchemy/SQLite/Encrypted backends. Google: documented manual conversation history pattern. For session-stateful agents, document loop-vs-terminate criteria in constraints.safety_boundaries.human_in_the_loop.

guardrails.description: Run input validation before agent processes user message; run output validation before returning result to caller
guardrails.implementation: parallel guardrail checks (OpenAI pattern) or pre/post hooks (Anthropic pattern)

cross_references._knowledge_files_format: Array of { path, purpose, runtime_strategy } objects pointing at knowledge files generated by make_agent_knowledge (typically in knowledge/). path = relative to repo root; purpose = one-line what-this-knowledge-is-for; runtime_strategy = 'embed' | 'read_at_runtime' | 'platform_cached' (matches the knowledge file's _metadata.runtime_strategy). KNW-QC-001..006 (rule 20 in make_agent_qc) validates that each referenced file exists and declares this agent in its consumed_by array.

changes: Added cross_references.knowledge_files[] field — array of { path, purpose, runtime_strategy } pointing at knowledge files generated by make_agent_knowledge. KNW-QC-001..006 (rule 20 in make_agent_qc) validates each reference.
Coordinates with new sibling skills: make_orchestrator_agent (generates multi_agent orchestrators) and make_agent_knowledge (generates knowledge/*.md+.json pairs for runtime reference).

Added Recommended Principle 'Choose a Memory/State Strategy Before Building' to make_agent.md (after Prefer MCP Servers). Three options: stateless, manual_history, platform_session. Source: Anthropic sessions + OpenAI Sessions primitive + Google manual conversation history convergence.
Added 'memory_state_strategy' entry to common_patterns.patterns with before/after contrast. Companion to the new principle.

Added Pitfall #10 'Lowering Temperature on Gemini 3 Models' to make_agent.md Common Pitfalls (former placeholder #10 renumbered to #11). Documents Google's explicit warning against lowering temperature on Gemini 3 — a direct exception to the wave-1 'Temperature by Agent Mode' principle.
Extended operational_guidance.best_practices.low_temperature_for_tool_agents description with the Gemini 3 carve-out so JSON-side readers see the exception alongside the original recommendation.

DEFERRED: Multi-Agent Handoffs principle + companion pattern (wave-2 proposals 1+2). Per user direction, this design space is large enough to deserve its own skill (make_orchestrator_agent or make_multi_agent) rather than a principle inside make_agent.md.

Added Recommended Principle 'Configure Reasoning Effort for Complex Tasks' to make_agent.md (after Temperature by Agent Mode). Source: Anthropic extended thinking + OpenAI reasoning_effort + xAI reasoning docs convergence.

Added Recommended Principle 'Prefer MCP Servers Over Custom Tools When Available' to make_agent.md (after Graceful Degradation). Source: Anthropic + OpenAI + Google MCP-as-first-class-integration convergence.

Added Pitfall #9 'Conflating Server Tools and Client Tools' to make_agent.md Common Pitfalls section (replaces former placeholder; placeholder renumbered to #10). Source: Anthropic tool-use docs distinguishing server-side vs. client-side execution.
Updated dedup table in Behavioral Discipline section to list the three new LLM-implementation principles.
